### 核心新功能  
1. **电脑控制**：  
   - 通过自身光标操作Mac应用（并行代理运行时不干扰你的任务，非常适合前端迭代和测试）。  
2. **网页与图像工具**：  
   - 内置浏览器支持直接页面批注（适用于前端开发和游戏制作；未来将实现完整浏览器控制）。  
   - 借助`gpt-image-1.5`生成并迭代视觉内容（产品概念、原型图等）。  
3. **开发流程提速**：  
   - 新增90+插件（包括Atlassian Jira、CircleCI、GitLab、微软办公套件等）。  
   - 支持GitHub PR评审、多终端、SSH远程开发环境（Alpha版）、文件预览（PDF/表格），以及代理计划摘要面板。  
4. **长期与个性化工作**：  
   - 可复用对话线程，支持跨天/周的任务日程安排。  
   - **记忆功能**：记住偏好和上下文，加速后续任务处理。  
   - **主动建议**：优先级行动列表（例如打开Google Docs批注、Slack/Notion上下文）。  


### 上线信息  
- 今日起向已通过ChatGPT登录的Codex桌面端用户逐步推送。  
- 个性化功能（建议/记忆）即将面向企业、教育用户及欧盟/英国用户推出。  
- 电脑控制功能初期仅支持macOS（欧盟/英国地区上线待定）。  


本次更新旨在缩小想象与实现之间的差距，更多功能正在规划中。  
[阅读完整文章](https://openai.com/news/codex-for-almost-everything)（2026年4月16日）。
items.content:
April 16, 2026

Product

Codex for (almost) everything

Loading…

We’re releasing a major update to Codex, making it a more powerful partner for the more than 3 million developers who use it every week to accelerate work across the full software development lifecycle.

Codex can now operate your computer alongside you, work with more of the tools and apps you use everyday, generate images, remember your preferences, learn from previous actions, and take on ongoing and repeatable work. The Codex app also now includes deeper support for developer workflows, like reviewing PRs, viewing multiple files & terminals, connecting to remote devboxes via SSH, and an in-app browser to make it faster to iterate on frontend designs, apps, and games. 

Extending Codex beyond coding

With background computer use, Codex can now use all of the apps on your computer by seeing, clicking, and typing with its own cursor. Multiple agents can work on your Mac in parallel, without interfering with your own work in other apps. For developers, this is helpful for iterating on frontend changes, testing apps, or working in apps that don’t expose an API.

Codex is also beginning to work natively with the web. The app now includes an in-app browser, where you can comment directly on pages to provide precise instructions to the agent. This is useful for frontend and game development today, and over time we plan to expand it so Codex can fully command the browser beyond web applications on localhost.

Codex can now use gpt-image-1.5⁠(opens in a new window) to generate and iterate on images. Combined with screenshots and code, it is helpful for creating visuals for product concepts, frontend designs, mockups, and games inside the same workflow.

We’re also releasing more than 90 additional plugins, which combine skills, app integrations, and MCP servers to give Codex more ways to gather context and take action across your tools. Some of the new plugins developers will find most useful include Atlassian Rovo to help manage JIRA, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, GitLab Issues, Microsoft Suite, Neon by Databricks, Remotion, Render, and Superpowers. 

Working across the software development lifecycle

The app now includes support for addressing GitHub review comments, running multiple terminal tabs, and connecting to remote devboxes over SSH in alpha. It also lets you open files directly in the sidebar with rich previews for PDFs, spreadsheets, slides, and docs, and use a new summary pane to track agent plans, sources, and artifacts.

Together, these improvements make it faster to move across all the stages of the software development lifecycle between writing code, checking outputs, reviewing changes, and collaborating with the agent in one workspace.

Carry work forward over time

We have expanded automations to allow re-using existing conversation threads, preserving context previously built up. Codex can now schedule future work for itself and wake up automatically to continue on a long-term task, potentially across days or weeks.

Teams use automations for everything from landing open pull requests to following up on tasks and staying on top of fast-moving conversations across tools like Slack, Gmail, and Notion.

We’re also releasing a preview of memory, which allows Codex to remember useful context from previous experience, including personal preferences, corrections and information that took time to gather. This helps future tasks complete faster and to a level of quality previously only possible through extensive custom instructions. 

Codex now also proactively proposes useful work to continue where you have left off. Using context from projects, connected plugins, and memory, Codex can now suggest how to start your work day or where to pick up on a previous project. For example Codex can identify open comments in Google Docs that require your attention, pull relevant context from Slack, Notion, and your codebase, then provide you with a prioritized list of actions.

Availability

Starting today, these updates are rolling out to Codex desktop app users who are signed in with ChatGPT.

Personalization features including context-aware suggestions and memory will roll out to Enterprise, Edu, and EU and UK users soon. Computer use is initially available on macOS, and will roll out to EU and UK users soon.

If you’ve been using Codex in the terminal or editor, try it across the rest of your workflow. If you haven’t tried Codex yet, download the app⁠ and get started.

What’s next

In just the year since Codex launched, the ways developers are using Codex has expanded. Developers start with Codex to write code, then increasingly use it to understand systems, gather context, review work, debug issues, coordinate with teammates, and keep longer-running work moving.

Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. That includes narrowing the gap between what people can imagine and what they can build. This release brings Codex closer to the tools, workflows, and decisions involved in building software, with much more to come soon.

  • Codex

Author

items.title: Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
items.titleZh: 隆重推出面向生命科学研究的GPT-Rosalind
items.summary: OpenAI has launched a specialized Life Sciences model to accelerate drug discovery and scientific research. Optimized for genomics, protein structure analysis, and drug design, it outperforms GPT-5.4 on 6/11 LABBench2 tasks. A free Codex plugin connects it to 50+ biology tools. Access starts with qualified US enterprise customers (trusted, safety-controlled), part of OpenAI’s long-term commitment to advancing life sciences AI.
items.summaryZh: OpenAI推出了一款生命科学专用模型，旨在加速药物研发与科学研究。该模型针对基因组学、蛋白质结构分析及药物设计进行了优化，在LABBench2的11项任务中，有6项表现优于GPT-5.4。一款免费的Codex插件可将其与50多种生物工具连接。该模型首先面向符合条件的美国企业客户开放（需通过信任与安全管控），这也是OpenAI长期致力于推进生命科学人工智能发展的一部分。
items.content:
April 16, 2026

ResearchRelease

Introducing GPT‑Rosalind for life sciences research

A new purpose-built model to accelerate scientific research and drug discovery.

Request accessLearn more

Today, we’re introducing GPT‑Rosalind, our frontier reasoning model built to support research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine. The life sciences model series is optimized for scientific workflows, combining improved tool use with deeper understanding across chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics.

On average, it takes roughly 10 to 15 years to go from target discovery to regulatory approval for a new drug in the United States. Gains made at the earliest stages of discovery compound downstream in better target selection, stronger biological hypotheses and higher-quality experiments. Progress in the life sciences is constrained not only by the difficulty of the underlying science, but by the complexity of the research workflows themselves. Scientists must work across large volumes of literature, specialized databases, experimental data, and evolving hypotheses in order to generate and evaluate new ideas. These workflows are often time-intensive, fragmented, and difficult to scale.

We believe advanced AI systems can help researchers move through these workflows faster—not just by making existing work more efficient, but by helping scientists explore more possibilities, surface connections that might otherwise be missed, and arrive at better hypotheses sooner. By supporting evidence synthesis, hypothesis generation, experimental planning, and other multi-step research tasks, this model is designed to help researchers accelerate the early stages of discovery. Over time, these systems could help life sciences organizations discover breakthroughs that wouldn’t otherwise be possible, with a much higher rate of success. 

GPT‑Rosalind is now available as a research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and the API for qualified customers through our trusted access program. We’re also introducing a freely accessible Life Sciences research plugin for Codex, helping scientists connect models to over 50 scientific tools and data sources. We are working with customers like Amgen, Moderna, the Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and others to apply GPT‑Rosalind across workflows that accelerate research and discovery.

The model is named after Rosalind Franklin, whose rigorous research helped reveal the structure of DNA and laid foundations for modern molecular biology.

From raw data to grounded discovery decisions, see how our purpose-built model accelerates research workflows.

Built for scientific workflows

The GPT‑Rosalind life sciences model series is built for modern scientific work across published evidence, data, tools, and experiments. In our evaluations, it delivers the best performance on tasks that require reasoning over molecules, proteins, genes, pathways, and disease-relevant biology, and it is more effective at using scientific tools and databases in multi-step workflows such as literature review, sequence-to-function interpretation, experimental planning, and data analysis.

This is the first release in our GPT‑Rosalind life sciences model series, and we will continue to expand the frontiers of the model’s biochemical reasoning capabilities across long-horizon, tool-heavy scientific workflows. OpenAI’s compute infrastructure gives us the ability to continue training, evaluating, and improving increasingly capable domain models against real scientific tasks—helping these systems become more useful as the workflows themselves become more complex.

From evidence-based discovery insights to high-impact experiments, see how our suite of solutions translate into measurable improvements in your research workflows.

Customers and ecosystem

We are working with leading pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and research customers, as well as life sciences technology organizations, to apply GPT‑Rosalind across workflows that drive discovery.

“The life sciences field demands precision at every step. The questions are highly complex, the data are highly unique, and the stakes are incredibly high. Our unique collaboration with OpenAI enables us to apply their most advanced capabilities and tools in new and innovative ways with the potential to accelerate how we deliver medicines to patients.”
—Sean Bruich, Senior Vice President of Artificial Intelligence and Data, Amgen

Performance and evaluation

We evaluated GPT‑Rosalind across a range of capabilities fundamental to scientific discovery and industry research. These evaluations measure core reasoning across scientific subdomains, including chemical reaction mechanisms; protein structure, mutation effects, and interactions; and phylogenetic interpretation of DNA sequences. They also assess whether models can support real research workflows by interpreting experimental outputs, identifying expert-relevant patterns, and synthesizing external information to design follow-up experiments. Finally, they test whether models can select and use the right computational tools, databases, and domain-specific capabilities to augment their reasoning. Taken together, these evaluations show progress across the end-to-end process of scientific research and suggest a stronger ability to help researchers work through challenging discovery tasks.

Prompt

I am planning a base-promoted SNAr coupling of 1-(pyridin-3-yl)ethanol with 1-fluoro-2-nitrobenzene with the goal of synthesizing 1-(pyridin-3-yl)ethyl 2-nitrophenyl ether. I found several patents that describe room-temperature O-arylation of alcohols in DMF/Cs2CO3, but the reaction is taking longer than I would like. How can I improve this reaction? Help me find any relevant literature or patents as well.

Industry evaluations

We evaluated GPT‑Rosalind on a series of public benchmarks. On BixBench, a benchmark designed around real-world bioinformatics and data analysis, GPT‑Rosalind achieved leading performance among models with published scores.

On LABBench2, a benchmark measuring performance on a range of research tasks such as literature retrieval, database access, sequence manipulation and protocol design, GPT‑Rosalind outperforms GPT‑5.4 on 6 out of 11 tasks. The most notable improvement comes from CloningQA, which requires end-to-end design of DNA and enzyme reagents for molecular cloning protocols.

We also partnered with Dyno Therapeutics, a company pioneering AI-designed gene therapies, to evaluate the model on an RNA sequence-to-function prediction and generation task using unpublished, uncontaminated sequences. Performance was compared against 57 historical scores from human experts in the AI-bio field. When evaluated directly in the Codex app, best-of-ten model submissions ranked above the 95th percentile of human experts on the prediction task and around the 84th percentile of human experts on the sequence generation task.

These evaluations provide a meaningful signal of performance on the kinds of workflows scientists rely on every day to generate evidence, analyze complex data, and move toward defensible biological conclusions.


Connecting to the tools scientists use

Scientists can use our new Life Sciences research plugin⁠(opens in a new window) for Codex, available today in GitHub. This package includes a broad set of modular skills for most common research workflows, designed to help users work across human genetics, functional genomics, protein structure, biochemistry, clinical evidence, and public study discovery.

These skills act as an orchestration layer that helps scientists work through broad, ambiguous, and multi-step questions more effectively. They provide access to more than 50 public multi-omics databases, literature sources, and biology tools, and offer a flexible starting point for common repeatable workflows such as protein structure lookup, sequence search, literature review, and public dataset discovery.

Eligible Enterprise users can leverage this plugin in research workflows with GPT‑Rosalind for deeper biological reasoning, while all users can use the plugin package with our mainline models.

Trusted access

We want to make these capabilities available to the scientists and research organizations best positioned to advance human health, while maintaining strong safeguards against biological misuse. The Life Sciences model is launching through a trusted-access deployment structure for qualified Enterprise customers in the U.S. to start, with controls around eligibility, access management, and organizational governance. At the same time, we are making a set of connectors and the Life Sciences Research Plugin available more broadly, so researchers can use our mainline models more effectively for life sciences research tasks. 

The Life Sciences model was developed with heightened enterprise-grade security controls and strengthened access management, enabling professional scientific use in governed research environments. We evaluate access based on three core principles: beneficial use, strong governance and safety oversight, and controlled access with enterprise-grade security. In practice, this means participating organizations must be conducting legitimate scientific research with clear public benefit; maintain appropriate governance, compliance, and misuse-prevention controls; and restrict access to approved users within secure, well-managed environments. Organizations must also agree to the life sciences research preview terms and comply with OpenAI’s usage policies, and we may request additional information as part of onboarding or continued participation.

Getting started

Organizations can request access⁠ through our qualification and safety review process.

During the research preview, use of this model will not consume existing credits or tokens—subject to abuse guardrails. We’ll share more details on pricing and availability as the program expands.

The Life Sciences model is built to help scientific organizations do higher-quality work, faster, in environments that require both technical capability and operational control. Our dedicated Life Sciences team—as well as advisory partners including McKinsey & Company, Boston Consulting Group (BCG), and Bain & Company—help organizations identify high-impact use cases, integrate the model into enterprise environments, and drive measurable outcomes. If you’d like to explore ways OpenAI Life Sciences can support your work, you can contact our Life Sciences team⁠.

What’s next

This is the first release in our Life Sciences model series, and we view it as the beginning of a long-term commitment to building AI that can accelerate scientific discovery in areas that matter deeply to society, from human health to broader biological research. We will continue improving the model’s biological reasoning, expanding support for tool-heavy and long-horizon research workflows, and working closely with leading scientific institutions to evaluate real-world impact. That includes ongoing partnerships with national laboratories such as Los Alamos National Laboratory, where we are exploring AI-guided protein and catalyst design, including the ability of AI systems to modify biological structures while preserving or improving key functional properties. 

Over time, we expect these systems to become increasingly capable partners in discovery—helping scientists move faster from question to evidence, from evidence to insight, and from insight to new treatments for patients.

items.content:
April 16, 2026

SecuritySafety

Accelerating the cyber defense ecosystem that protects us all

Loading…

Trusted Access for Cyber⁠ is designed around a simple premise: advanced cyber capabilities should reach defenders broadly, but access should scale with trust, validation, and safeguards. Today we’re sharing the first organizations helping put that approach into practice, from open-source security teams and vulnerability researchers to enterprises operating some of the world’s most complex digital environments.

The strength of this approach comes from the breadth of defenders involved. Cybersecurity is a team sport, and the systems people rely on are protected by organizations of many kinds, from major enterprises and security vendors to researchers, maintainers, public institutions, nonprofits, and smaller teams with limited security resources.

Investing in the broader ecosystem

Not every organization has the benefit of a 24x7 security team who is able to respond to incidents when they are disclosed on a Friday night⁠(opens in a new window). It’s important for all software developers to benefit from the advanced cybersecurity capabilities of frontier models, which is why we’ve committed $10 million in API credits through our Cybersecurity Grant Program. 

Initial recipients include Socket⁠(opens in a new window) and Semgrep⁠(opens in a new window), which focus on software supply chain security, and Calif⁠(opens in a new window) and Trail of Bits⁠(opens in a new window), which pair frontier models with vulnerability research experts. We’re looking to partner with more teams that have a proven track record of identifying and remediating vulnerabilities in open source software and critical infrastructure systems—teams can apply here⁠⁠. 

Building resilience together

The critical defenders joining this program protect digital infrastructure we all rely on, and will help us learn from real-world use, improve our safety systems, and make advanced defensive capabilities more useful across the ecosystem. These are firms that are already world-renowned for enterprise security leadership in their respective industries. The goal is to build the trust, verification, and accountability needed to make these tools available to the many defenders whose work keeps people, institutions, and critical systems safe.

Companies and organizations that have already signed up to support these efforts include Bank of America, BlackRock, BNY, Citi, Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, Goldman Sachs, iVerify, JPMorgan Chase, Morgan Stanley, NVIDIA, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, SpecterOps, and Zscaler⁠(opens in a new window). 

We have also provided access to GPT‑5.4‑Cyber to the U.S. Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) and the UK AI Security Institute (UK AISI) so that they can conduct evaluations focused on the model’s cyber capabilities and safeguards.

We will keep expanding Trusted Access for Cyber as we learn, with safeguards that rise with capability and pathways that help legitimate defenders move faster. We want participants to push the frontier of defensive research, share what they discover, and help turn new insights into stronger protection for everyone. Cyber defense is a shared challenge, and this program should reflect the breadth of the people and organizations doing that work every day.

“BNY is committed to helping protect the security and resilience of the financial system as AI capabilities accelerate. We are working closely with those at the forefront of enabling these efforts. Building on our ongoing collaboration with OpenAI, we are pleased to participate in their Trusted Access for Cyber program”
—Leigh-Ann Russell, Chief Information Officer and Global Head of Engineering
  • 2026

Author

OpenAI

items.summary:
This is a WeChat environment verification page designed to address an abnormal access state. Key details include:  

- **Core Message**: It displays an error prompt ("Current environment is abnormal; complete verification to continue accessing") with a "Go to verify" button.  
- **Technical Components**:  
  - WeUI (WeChat’s UI framework) styles supporting light/dark/care modes and accessibility features.  
  - Scripts for debugging (VConsole, triggered via cookies/URL params), Tencent Captcha (TCaptcha) integration, and verification logic.  
  - CGI data containing parameters like the target URL (original content page), captcha app ID, session IDs, and timeout for the verification process.  

The page facilitates user verification to restore access to the intended WeChat content.

items.name: forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity
* **Mission Statement:** A single `CLAUDE.md` file (and Claude Code plugin) delivering actionable guidelines derived from Andrej Karpathy’s observations to fix common LLM coding pitfalls in Claude Code.
* **Target Problem:** LLM behaviors like unstated assumptions, overcomplication, orthogonal code edits, and lack of verifiable success criteria when generating code.

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators
* **Core Innovation:** Four targeted principles (Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution) directly addressing Karpathy’s identified pitfalls, packaged for Claude Code.
* **Comparison:** Unlike generic guidelines, it’s tailored to Claude’s specific coding flaws (e.g., silent assumption picking) and offers plugin/per-project deployment for reusability.

## 3. Practical Utility
* **Key Features:** 1) Claude Code plugin for cross-project use; 2) Per-project `CLAUDE.md` (curlable); 3) Customizable with project-specific rules; 4) Focus on verifiable success criteria (tests-first loops).
items.summaryZh:
---

## 1. 项目定位
* **使命陈述**：一份单一的`CLAUDE.md`文件（及Claude Code插件），基于Andrej Karpathy的观察提供可落地的指南，以解决Claude Code中常见的大语言模型编码陷阱。
* **目标问题**：大语言模型生成代码时的常见行为问题，包括隐含假设、过度复杂化、无关代码修改，以及缺乏可验证的成功标准。

## 2. 创新与差异化
* **核心创新**：「编码前思考」「简化优先」「精准修改」「目标导向执行」四项针对性原则，直接解决Karpathy指出的问题，并针对Claude Code进行适配。
* **对比优势**：与通用指南不同，本项目针对Claude的特定编码缺陷（如静默选择假设）定制，支持插件/项目级部署以实现复用。

## 3. 实用价值
* **核心特性**：1）支持跨项目使用的Claude Code插件；2）可通过curl获取的项目级`CLAUDE.md`文件；3）支持自定义项目特定规则；4）聚焦可验证的成功标准（测试先行循环）。

items.name: VoltAgent/awesome-design-md
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
* **Mission Statement**: Curated collection of Google Stitch-compliant DESIGN.md (plain markdown) files enabling AI agents to generate UI matching real-world websites.  
* **Target Problem**: Eliminates complex design tooling (Figma exports, JSON schemas) and manual adjustments to align AI-generated interfaces with desired design systems.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
* **Core Innovation**: Uses markdown (LLM-native format) for design systems, with pre-built files from 68+ popular sites (no parsing/configuration required).  
* **Comparison**: Unlike industry standards (Figma, JSON), it leverages plain text for universal AI compatibility and provides ready-to-use assets from real-world examples.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
* **Key Features**:  
  1. 68+ DESIGN.md files (AI, dev tools, fintech, etc.) with color, typography, component, and responsive rules.  
  2. Light/dark preview HTMLs for visual validation.  
  3. AI prompt guides to simplify integration.  
  4. Drop-in compatibility with Google Stitch and AI coding agents.
items.summaryZh:

* **使命陈述**：精选符合Google Stitch规范的DESIGN.md（纯Markdown）文件集合，助力AI代理生成与真实网站匹配的UI界面。  
* **核心痛点**：消除复杂设计工具链（Figma导出、JSON Schema）及手动调整，让AI生成界面直接匹配目标设计系统。  

### 2. 创新与差异化  
* **核心创新**：采用Markdown（大语言模型原生格式）构建设计系统，内置68+热门网站预生成文件（无需解析/配置）。  
* **对比优势**：不同于行业主流（Figma、JSON），以纯文本实现AI通用兼容，提供真实案例的即用型资源。  

### 3. 实用价值  
* **核心特性**：  
  1. 68+份DESIGN.md文件（覆盖AI、开发工具、金融科技等领域），包含色彩、排版、组件及响应式规则；  
  2. 明暗模式预览HTML，支持视觉验证；  
  3. AI提示词指南，简化集成流程；  
  4. 与Google Stitch及AI coding代理无缝兼容。

items.name: obra/superpowers
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** A plugin ecosystem for AI coding assistants (Codex, OpenCode.ai, Claude Code) delivering reusable skills, cross-platform integration, and workflow improvements.  
**Target Problem:** AI assistants lack standardized, tool-specific skills; cross-platform compatibility gaps and sandboxed environment limitations (e.g., Codex App) hinder consistent coding workflows.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** Polyglot cross-platform hooks (CMD/bash compatible), shared skill discovery core (`lib/skills-core.js` for multi-tool support), non-blocking visual brainstorming (browser + terminal), zero-dependency Node server (RFC 6455 WebSocket).  
**Comparison:** Unlike generic skill libraries, Superpowers is tool-tailored with cross-platform hooks, shared core for consistency, and sandbox-aware workflows (Codex App worktree adaptation).  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. Cross-platform AI plugin hooks (polyglot wrappers).  
2. Reusable skill library (multi-tool support via shared core).  
3. Non-blocking visual brainstorming (browser + terminal).  
4. Workflow enhancements (document review loops, Codex App sandbox compatibility).
items.summaryZh:
### 1. 项目定位  
**核心使命**：为AI代码助手（Codex、OpenCode.ai、Claude Code）构建插件生态系统，提供可复用技能、跨平台集成及工作流优化。  
**待解决问题**：AI助手缺乏标准化的工具专属技能；跨平台兼容性缺口及沙盒环境限制（如Codex App），阻碍了连贯的编码工作流。  

### 2. 创新点与差异化优势  
**核心创新**：多语言跨平台钩子（兼容CMD/bash）、共享技能发现核心（`lib/skills-core.js`支持多工具）、非阻塞式可视化头脑风暴（浏览器+终端）、无依赖Node服务器（遵循RFC 6455的WebSocket）。  
**对比优势**：与通用技能库不同，Superpowers针对工具定制，具备跨平台钩子、共享核心（确保一致性）及支持沙盒环境的工作流（适配Codex App工作树）。  

### 3. 实用价值  
**核心功能**：  
1. 跨平台AI插件钩子（多语言封装）  
2. 可复用技能库（通过共享核心支持多工具）  
3. 非阻塞式可视化头脑风暴（浏览器+终端）  
4. 工作流增强（文档审核循环、Codex App沙盒兼容性）

items.name: NawfalMotii79/PLFM_RADAR
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
* **Mission Statement:** Open-source phased array radar system (AERIS-10) with FPGA-based signal processing and comprehensive hardware/software implementation documentation.  
* **Target Problem:** Addresses the gap in accessible open-source phased array radar designs with end-to-end implementation details for FPGA platforms.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
* **Core Innovation:** End-to-end open-source phased array radar with detailed hardware bring-up (checklists, board workflows) and FPGA signal processing (MTI, CA-CFAR) for Trenz TE0713/TE0701 boards.  
* **Comparison:** Unlike closed-source industry alternatives, it provides transparent documentation (architecture, build logs, timing reports) and open access to radar signal processing blocks.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
* **Key Features:**  
  1. FPGA-based signal processing pipeline (ADC → DDC → MTI → Doppler → CA-CFAR).  
  2. Detailed hardware bring-up checklists and board-day worksheets.  
  3. FT601 USB interface for host communication.  
  4. Implementation logs with build histories and resource utilization reports.

items.name: Lordog/dive-into-llms
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** A free, open-source hands-on programming tutorial series for learning large language models (LLMs) with practical experiments and ready-to-run code.  
**Target Problem:** Students/researchers lack accessible, hands-on LLM resources covering both foundational and advanced topics (e.g., safety, alignment, multi-modal) with working code.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** Combines SJTU academic course materials with Jupyter notebooks for 11+ LLM topics (tuning, prompting, jailbreaking, RLHF) plus a localized Huawei Ascend series for domestic hardware.  
**Comparison:** Unlike basic API-focused tutorials, it offers deep, actionable experiments (e.g., steganography, GUI agents) with code, plus localized content for Huawei’s Ascend platform.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. Curated Jupyter notebooks for 11 LLM topics (tuning, safety, multi-modal).  
2. Joint Huawei Ascend series for localized hardware compatibility.  
3. Covers advanced topics (RLHF, jailbreaking, steganography) with step-by-step guides.  
4. Free, community-contributed, and aligned with academic research.

items.name: shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice
items.summary:
# Claude Code Best Practice: A Community-Curated Hub for AI-Assisted Development

## What is this repo?
A **community-curated resource hub** for **Claude Code** (Anthropic's AI-assisted development tool) that aggregates **best practices, expert tips, curated workflows, comparative reports, and learning resources** to help teams optimize their AI-assisted development workflow.


## Key Content & Value
### 1. **Core Concepts Deep Dive**  
Covers Claude Code's building blocks with actionable guidance:  
- **Subagents**: Isolated context for focused tasks (e.g., research, debugging).  
- **Commands**: Reusable, git-tracked workflows (e.g., `/weather-orchestrator`).  
- **Skills**: Configurable knowledge modules with progressive disclosure.  
- **Hooks**: Event handlers for automation (e.g., auto-formatting, permission checks).  
- **Memory**: Best practices for `CLAUDE.md` (context management) and avoiding "context rot."  

### 2. **Expert Tips from the Claude Team**  
Practical advice from creators (Boris Cherny, Thariq) and community leaders:  
- Use **plan mode** for complex tasks (Opus for planning, Sonnet for coding).  
- **Subagents** for context isolation (keep main context clean).  
- **Auto mode** (model-based permission classifier) to reduce prompts.  
- **Squash merging** (small PRs = 118-line p50) for clean history.  

### 3. **Curated Workflows**  
Aggregated workflows from top Claude Code repos (e.g., *Everything Claude Code*, *Superpowers*) with:  
- Uniqueness scores (e.g., TDD-first, spec-driven).  
- Component breakdowns (agents/commands/skills used).  

### 4. **Comparative Reports**  
Side-by-side analyses of Claude Code features:  
- **Agent SDK vs CLI**: When to use each for development.  
- **Browser Automation**: Claude in Chrome vs Chrome DevTools MCP.  
- **Global vs Project Settings**: Optimizing configuration.  

### 5. **Learning Resources**  
- Videos/podcasts with the Claude team (e.g., Boris Cherny on building Claude Code).  
- Links to official docs, related repos (e.g., `claude-code-hooks`), and community resources.  


## Why it matters
- **Saves time**: Aggregates scattered best practices into one place.  
- **Actionable**: Provides tested, expert-endorsed techniques (not just theory).  
- **Scalable**: Helps teams standardize AI-assisted development workflows.  
- **Insightful**: Comparative reports and open questions (e.g., "When to use agents vs commands?") guide decision-making.  


## Who it's for
- **Developers**: Using Claude Code for coding, debugging, or automation.  
- **Teams**: Looking to scale AI-assisted development with standardized practices.  
- **Learners**: Wanting to master advanced Claude Code features and expert techniques.  


This repo is a **must-have** for anyone serious about leveraging Claude Code to its full potential—combining official knowledge, team insights, and community wisdom into a single, accessible resource.
items.summaryZh:
# Claude Code 最佳实践：AI辅助开发社区精选资源中心

## 这是什么仓库？
这是一个**社区精选资源中心**，针对Anthropic的AI辅助开发工具Claude Code，汇总了**最佳实践、专家技巧、精选工作流、对比报告及学习资源**，帮助团队优化AI辅助开发流程。


## 核心内容与价值
### 1. **核心概念深度解析**  
覆盖Claude Code的核心组件及 actionable 指导：  
- **子代理**：专注任务的隔离上下文（如研究、调试）。  
- **命令**：可复用、Git跟踪的工作流（如 `/weather-orchestrator`）。  
- **技能**：支持渐进式披露的可配置知识模块。  
- **钩子**：自动化事件处理器（如自动格式化、权限检查）。  
- **记忆**：`CLAUDE.md`（上下文管理）最佳实践及避免「上下文腐烂」的方法。  

### 2. **Claude团队专家技巧**  
来自 creators（Boris Cherny、Thariq）及社区领袖的实用建议：  
- 复杂任务用**计划模式**（Opus规划、Sonnet编码）。  
- **子代理**隔离上下文（保持主上下文整洁）。  
- 启用**自动模式**（基于模型的权限分类器）减少Prompt数量。  
- 用**squash合并**（小PR的p50为118行）保持提交历史整洁。  

### 3. **精选工作流**  
汇总自顶级Claude Code仓库（如《Everything Claude Code》《Superpowers》）的工作流，包含：  
- 独特性评分（如测试驱动优先、规范驱动）。  
- 组件拆解（使用的代理/命令/技能）。  

### 4. **对比报告**  
Claude Code功能的 side-by-side 分析：  
- **Agent SDK vs CLI**：开发中何时用各自。  
- **浏览器自动化**：Chrome中的Claude vs Chrome DevTools MCP。  
- **全局设置 vs 项目设置**：优化配置。  

### 5. **学习资源**  
- Claude团队的视频/播客（如Boris Cherny讲解Claude Code构建）。  
- 官方文档、相关仓库（如`claude-code-hooks`）及社区资源链接。  


## 重要性
- **节省时间**：分散的最佳实践汇总至一处。  
- **可落地**：提供经测试、专家认可的技巧（非纯理论）。  
- **可扩展**：帮助团队标准化AI辅助开发流程。  
- **有洞察**：对比报告及开放性问题（如「何时用代理 vs 命令？」）辅助决策。  


## 适用人群
- **开发者**：用Claude Code编码、调试或自动化。  
- **团队**：希望通过标准化实践扩展AI辅助开发。  
- **学习者**：掌握Claude Code高级功能及专家技巧。  


对于希望充分发挥Claude Code潜力的人，这是必备仓库——整合官方知识、团队见解及社区智慧，成为单一易用的资源。

items.name: thedotmack/claude-mem
items.summary:
Claude-mem is a **persistent memory tool for AI sessions** that captures, structures, and retrieves context (tool usage, file edits, decisions) to enhance future AI interactions. It works across platforms like Claude Desktop, Cursor IDE, and Gemini CLI.


### **Core Features**  
1. **Persistent Context Storage**: Captures session events (tool runs, edits, prompts) and processes them into structured observations (titles, narratives, facts) for long-term retention.  
2. **Token-Efficient Search**: Uses **MCP Tools** (search, timeline, get_observations) with a **3-layer workflow** (index → context → full details) to retrieve relevant memory without wasting tokens.  
3. **Multi-AI Provider Support**: Works with Claude, Gemini, and OpenRouter for observation extraction (supports free/paid models).  
4. **Privacy & Control**:  
   - **Private Tags**: Exclude sensitive content (API keys, personal notes) from storage using `<private>` tags.  
   - **Export/Import**: Share memories across installations via JSON files.  
5. **Cross-Tool Integration**: Integrates with Claude Desktop, Cursor IDE, and Gemini CLI.


### **Technical Stack**  
- **Languages**: TypeScript  
- **Runtime**: Bun/Node.js  
- **Storage**: SQLite (with FTS5 for full-text search) + Chroma Vector DB (semantic search)  
- **API**: Express.js (worker service for processing/querying)  


### **Key Components**  
1. **Plugin Hooks**: Capture session events (e.g., tool usage, file edits) from the host AI tool.  
2. **Worker Service**: Background service to process observations, manage queues, and serve API requests.  
3. **Database Layer**: Stores structured observations, session summaries, and user prompts.  
4. **MCP Tools**: CLI/API tools for searching and retrieving memory (token-efficient workflow).


### **Use Cases**  
- Retrieve past solutions/decisions for a project.  
- Avoid redoing work by leveraging previous context.  
- Share knowledge across team members via export/import.  
- Control what’s stored with private tags for sensitive content.


For more details, refer to the [official docs](https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem/tree/main/docs/public).
items.summaryZh:
Claude-mem是一款AI会话持久化记忆工具，可捕获、结构化并检索上下文（工具使用、文件编辑、决策），提升后续AI交互体验。它兼容Claude Desktop、Cursor IDE及Gemini CLI等多平台。


### **核心功能**  
1. **持久化上下文存储**：捕获会话事件（工具调用、文件编辑、提示词），并处理为结构化观测数据（标题、描述、事实）以实现长期留存。  
2. **高效Token搜索**：借助MCP工具（搜索、时间线、获取观测数据）和三层工作流（索引→上下文→完整详情），精准检索相关记忆且不浪费Token。  
3. **多AI提供商支持**：兼容Claude、Gemini及OpenRouter，支持观测数据提取（免费/付费模型均可）。  
4. **隐私与可控性**：  
   - **私密标签**：使用`<private>`标签排除敏感内容（API密钥、个人笔记）的存储。  
   - **导出/导入**：通过JSON文件在不同安装环境间共享记忆。  
5. **跨工具集成**：兼容Claude Desktop、Cursor IDE及Gemini CLI。


### **技术栈**  
- **开发语言**：TypeScript  
- **运行时**：Bun/Node.js  
- **存储**：SQLite（带FTS5全文检索）+ Chroma向量数据库（语义检索）  
- **API框架**：Express.js（处理/查询的Worker服务）  


### **核心组件**  
1. **插件钩子**：从宿主AI工具捕获会话事件（如工具使用、文件编辑）。  
2. **Worker服务**：后台服务，负责处理观测数据、管理队列及响应API请求。  
3. **数据库层**：存储结构化观测数据、会话摘要及用户提示词。  
4. **MCP工具**：用于搜索和检索记忆的CLI/API工具（高效Token工作流）。


### **应用场景**  
- 检索项目过往解决方案/决策。  
- 借助历史上下文避免重复工作。  
- 通过导出/导入实现团队成员间的知识共享。  
- 使用私密标签管控敏感内容的存储。


更多详情请参考[官方文档](https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem/tree/main/docs/public)。

items.name: JuliusBrussee/caveman
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** A semantic constraint engine for Claude Code/Codex that minimizes token usage while retaining technical accuracy.  
**Target Problem:** Excess conversational padding in LLM outputs increases token latency/cost without preserving critical technical content for engineering tasks.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** Lithic Token Compression—uses a lexical ruleset to eliminate non-technical padding, retain code/imperatives, and cut tokens by ~65%.  
**Comparison:** Unlike generic tokenizers, it’s domain-specific for software engineering (Claude Code/Codex) and prioritizes technical accuracy over raw token count.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. ~65% average token latency reduction for standard engineering tasks.  
2. Preserves code blocks/technical imperatives during compression.  
3. Install via `npx skills add JuliusBrussee/caveman`.  
4. Trigger with `/caveman` command.
items.summaryZh:
### 1. 项目定位  
**项目使命**：针对Claude Code/Codex的语义约束引擎，可在保留技术准确性的前提下，最大程度减少Token使用量。  
**核心问题**：大语言模型（LLM）输出中过多的对话式冗余内容，会增加Token延迟与成本，却无法保留工程任务所需的关键技术内容。  

### 2. 创新点与差异化优势  
**核心创新**：Lithic Token压缩技术——采用词法规约集，消除非技术冗余内容、保留代码与指令性语句，Token数量减少约65%。  
**对比优势**：与通用Tokenizer不同，本技术针对软件工程领域（Claude Code/Codex）定制，优先保障技术准确性而非单纯追求Token数量。  

### 3. 实用价值  
**核心功能**：  
1. 标准工程任务下，Token延迟平均降低约65%；  
2. 压缩过程中保留代码块与技术指令；  
3. 安装方式：通过`npx skills add JuliusBrussee/caveman`命令安装；  
4. 触发方式：使用`/caveman`命令触发。

items.name: lsdefine/GenericAgent
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement**: A minimal, self-evolving autonomous agent framework that empowers LLMs with system-level control over local computers (browser, terminal, etc.) and grows reusable skills over time.  
**Target Problem**: Existing agents are complex (large codebases), lack self-evolution, use sandboxed browsers (lose sessions), or have high token costs.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation**: Automatic skill crystallization—each task’s execution path becomes a reusable skill, forming a personal skill tree.  
**Comparison**: 3k-line core (vs 530k+ for OpenClaw), real browser control (preserves sessions), 30k context window (vs 200k-1M), self-evolving (vs stateless/ plugin-based), and self-bootstrap proof (built itself).  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features**:  
1. Self-evolving skill tree (grows with use);  
2. Minimal 3k-line core + 100-line agent loop;  
3. Real browser control (preserves login sessions);  
4. Multi-model support (Claude/Gemini/Kimi) + bot frontends (WeChat/QQ/Telegram).
items.summaryZh:
## 1. 项目定位  
**使命陈述**：轻量自进化自主智能体框架，赋予大语言模型对本地计算机（浏览器、终端等）的系统级控制权，并随使用积累可复用技能。  
**核心痛点**：现有智能体存在代码库庞大（复杂）、缺乏自进化能力、使用沙盒浏览器（丢失会话）或 token 成本高等问题。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
**核心创新**：技能自动固化——每个任务的执行路径转化为可复用技能，形成个人技能树。  
**对比优势**：核心代码仅3000行（OpenClaw超53万行）、真实浏览器控制（保留会话）、3万上下文窗口（对比20万-100万）、自进化（对比无状态/插件式），且具备自举验证能力（可自我构建）。  

## 3. 实用价值  
**核心特性**：  
1. 自进化技能树（随使用增长）；  
2. 轻量核心（3000行）+ 100行智能体循环；  
3. 真实浏览器控制（保留登录会话）；  
4. 多模型支持（Claude/Gemini/Kimi）+ 多聊天机器人前端（微信/QQ/Telegram）。

items.name: firecrawl/open-lovable
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** Enable automatic package installation and command execution from AI-generated code responses in sandbox environments (Vercel/E2B) using XML tagging, with real-time user feedback.  
**Target Problem:** AI models (e.g., Groq) often lack tool call support, breaking traditional package installation workflows; users need visibility into sandbox operations.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** XML-based package detection (instead of tool calls) for AI responses, including buffering for streaming chunks and SSE-powered real-time UI feedback.  
**Comparison:** Unlike tool call-dependent solutions (Vercel AI SDK), works with models without tool support; adds granular, real-time progress updates (missing in standard tool call flows).  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. XML tag parsing (`<package>`/`<packages>`) for AI-generated package lists.  
2. SSE-streamed real-time feedback (package installs, command execution).  
3. Streaming API fixes (null checks, array initialization) for robust error handling.  
4. Compatibility with Vercel/E2B sandboxes for end-to-end code execution.
items.summaryZh:
## 1. 项目定位  
**使命陈述**：借助XML标记，在沙盒环境（Vercel/E2B）中实现AI生成代码响应的自动包安装与命令执行，并提供实时用户反馈。  
**核心问题**：AI模型（如Groq）常缺乏工具调用支持，导致传统包安装流程中断；用户需了解沙盒操作的执行情况。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
**核心创新**：针对AI响应采用基于XML的包检测（而非工具调用），包含流块缓冲与SSE驱动的实时UI反馈。  
**对比优势**：与依赖工具调用的解决方案（Vercel AI SDK）不同，本方案支持无工具调用能力的模型；补充了精细的实时进度更新（标准工具调用流程中缺失此功能）。  

## 3. 实用价值  
**核心功能**：  
1. 解析AI生成包列表的XML标签（`<package>`/`<packages>`）  
2. SSE流式传输实时反馈（包安装、命令执行）  
3. 修复流式API问题（空值检查、数组初始化），实现稳健错误处理  
4. 兼容Vercel/E2B沙盒，支持端到端代码执行

items.name: pascalorg/editor
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
* **Mission Statement:** A modular 3D building editor built with React Three Fiber and WebGPU for interactive architectural design.  
* **Target Problem:** Solves inefficiencies in 3D scene state management, real-time geometry updates, and modular extensibility for architectural tools.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
* **Core Innovation:** Flat node storage (parent-child via IDs) + scene registry for O(1) 3D object lookup; per-package Zustand stores (core/viewer/editor) with dirty node system for incremental updates.  
* **Comparison:** Unlike nested-state 3D editors, uses flat nodes + registry for fast access; WebGPU outperforms WebGL; modular packages enable reusability (e.g., viewer for embedded use).  

## 3. Practical Utility  
* **Key Features:**  
  1. Modular monorepo (core: state/systems, viewer: rendering, editor: UI/tools)  
  2. Zustand + Zundo: Persistent scene state with 50-step undo/redo  
  3. Real-time geometry systems (wall/slab/roof) using dirty nodes  
  4. Typed event bus for inter-component communication (node clicks, placement)
items.summaryZh:
## 1. 项目定位  
* **使命陈述**：一款基于React Three Fiber与WebGPU开发的模块化3D建筑编辑器，支持交互式建筑设计。  
* **核心问题**：解决建筑工具在3D场景状态管理、实时几何更新及模块化扩展性方面的效率痛点。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
* **核心创新**：采用扁平节点存储（通过ID关联父子关系）+场景注册表，实现O(1)复杂度的3D对象快速查找；针对核心、查看器、编辑器模块分别使用Zustand状态管理，结合脏节点系统实现增量更新。  
* **对比优势**：不同于嵌套状态的3D编辑器，扁平节点+注册表设计大幅提升访问速度；WebGPU性能优于WebGL；模块化包支持复用（如查看器可嵌入其他场景）。  

## 3. 实用价值  
* **核心功能**：  
  1. 模块化单体仓库：核心（状态/系统）、查看器（渲染）、编辑器（界面/工具）分离  
  2. Zustand + Zundo：持久化场景状态，支持50步撤销/重做  
  3. 基于脏节点的实时几何系统：支持墙、板、屋顶等构件  
  4. 类型化事件总线：实现节点点击、放置等组件间通信

items.name: PurpleAILAB/Decepticon
items.summary:
# Decepticon: Autonomous Attack-Defense Platform Summary

## Core Vision: The Offensive Vaccine
Decepticon implements an **Offensive Vaccine**—a continuous hardening loop where:
1. **Offensive Agents** (red team) simulate attacks to find vulnerabilities.
2. **Defensive Agents** apply automated fixes (patches, WAF rules, config changes).
3. The loop repeats to strengthen defenses over time, creating a self-healing system.


## Key Architecture & Components
### 1. Dual-Lane Architecture
- **Offensive Lane**: Recon, Exploit, Post-Exploit agents (e.g., Nmap, Nuclei, SQLMap).
- **Defensive Lane**: WAF, Patch, Config, Detection agents (e.g., ModSecurity rule management).

### 2. Critical Components
- **Ralph Loop Engine**: Orchestrates the continuous cycle (Recon → Attack → Finding → Feedback → Defense → Verify).
- **AttackDefensePlanAdapter**: Integrates attack (OPPLAN) and defense plans into a unified workflow.
- **Feedback Pipeline**: Converts offensive findings into actionable defensive remediations.
- **Knowledge Graph**: Stores attack paths, vulnerabilities, and defensive actions for context-aware decision-making.
- **OpenClaw Integration**: Uses OpenClaw's gateway (WebSocket/HTTP), agent runtime, and plugin system for scalability.

### 3. OPSEC & Security
- **Docker Sandboxes**: Isolate agents to prevent real-system impact.
- **Allowlist System**: Restrict tool access to safe paths (e.g., `/workspace/evidence`).
- **SSRF Protection**: Mitigate server-side request forgery risks.


## How It Works (Continuous Hardening Loop)
1. **Recon**: Offensive agents gather target info (subdomains, open ports, services).
2. **Attack**: Exploit agents test for vulnerabilities (SQLi, XSS, RCE).
3. **Finding**: Extract structured vulnerabilities (CWE, CVSS, MITRE ATT&CK mappings).
4. **Feedback**: Convert findings into defensive actions (e.g., "Add WAF rule for SQLi").
5. **Defense**: Apply fixes via defensive agents (patch servers, update WAF rules).
6. **Verify**: Re-test to confirm vulnerabilities are mitigated.
7. **Convergence**: Measure progress with metrics (findings count, immunity rate, severity distribution).


## Key Use Cases
- **Continuous Security Testing**: Automate red teaming to find vulnerabilities 24/7.
- **Self-Healing Defenses**: Fix issues without manual intervention.
- **Purple Teaming**: Align red/blue teams via shared knowledge graph and feedback loops.


## Core Concepts Recap
| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Offensive Vaccine** | Digital vaccine using simulated attacks to strengthen defenses. |
| **Ralph Loop** | Autonomous cycle of attack → defense → verification. |
| **Finding** | Structured vulnerability data (CWE, CVSS, MITRE). |
| **RemediationAction** | Defensive fix derived from a finding (e.g., patch, WAF rule). |
| **AttackDefensePlanAdapter** | Unifies attack/defense plans for the Ralph Loop. |

Decepticon bridges offensive red teaming and defensive security to create a self-improving security system, reducing manual effort and improving defense resilience.
items.summaryZh:
# Decepticon：自主攻防平台概述

## 核心愿景：进攻性疫苗
Decepticon 采用「进攻性疫苗」机制——一种持续强化的闭环，具体流程为：
1. **进攻方代理**（红队）模拟攻击以发现漏洞；
2. **防守方代理**自动应用修复（补丁、WAF规则、配置变更）；
3. 闭环持续迭代，逐步强化防御，最终形成自愈系统。


## 核心架构与组件
### 1. 双轨架构
- **进攻轨**：侦察、漏洞利用、后漏洞利用代理（例如 Nmap、Nuclei、SQLMap）；
- **防守轨**：WAF、补丁、配置、检测代理（例如 ModSecurity 规则管理）。

### 2. 关键组件
- **Ralph 循环引擎**：协调持续循环（侦察→攻击→发现→反馈→防御→验证）；
- **攻防计划适配器**：将攻击计划（OPLAN）与防御计划整合为统一工作流；
- **反馈流水线**：将进攻方发现转化为可执行的防御修复措施；
- **知识图谱**：存储攻击路径、漏洞及防御动作，支持上下文感知决策；
- **OpenClaw 集成**：利用 OpenClaw 的网关（WebSocket/HTTP）、代理运行时及插件系统实现可扩展性。

### 3. OPSEC（操作安全）与自身安全
- **Docker 沙箱**：隔离代理，避免影响真实系统；
- **白名单系统**：限制工具仅能访问安全路径（例如 `/workspace/evidence`）；
- **SSRF 防护**：缓解服务器端请求伪造风险。


## 工作原理（持续强化闭环）
1. **侦察**：进攻方代理收集目标信息（子域名、开放端口、服务）；
2. **攻击**：漏洞利用代理测试漏洞（SQL注入、跨站脚本、远程代码执行）；
3. **发现**：提取结构化漏洞数据（CWE、CVSS、MITRE ATT&CK 映射）；
4. **反馈**：将发现转化为防御动作（例如「添加 SQL注入防护的 WAF 规则」）；
5. **防御**：通过防守方代理应用修复（打补丁、更新 WAF 规则）；
6. **验证**：重新测试以确认漏洞已缓解；
7. **收敛**：通过指标衡量进展（发现数量、免疫率、严重程度分布）。


## 核心应用场景
- **持续安全测试**：自动化红队作业，7×24 小时发现漏洞；
- **自愈防御**：无需人工干预即可修复问题；
- **紫队协作**：通过共享知识图谱与反馈闭环协调红队与蓝队。


## 核心概念回顾
| 术语                | 定义说明                                  |
|---------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| **进攻性疫苗**      | 利用模拟攻击强化防御的数字疫苗            |
| **Ralph 循环**      | 攻击→防御→验证的自主循环                  |
| **发现**            | 结构化漏洞数据（CWE、CVSS、MITRE）        |
| **修复动作**        | 从漏洞发现衍生的防御修复（例如补丁、WAF规则） |
| **攻防计划适配器**  | 为 Ralph 循环统一攻击与防御计划            |

Decepticon 打通红队进攻与蓝队防御，打造自主进化的安全系统，减少人工投入并提升防御韧性。

items.name: jamiepine/voicebox
items.summary:
**Voicebox** is an open-source, local-first voice cloning and text-to-speech (TTS) studio designed as a privacy-focused alternative to cloud-based tools like ElevenLabs. Key capabilities include:  

### Core Features  
1. **Voice Cloning**: Clone voices from 10–30 second audio clips using models like Qwen3-TTS (captures prosody/emotion).  
2. **Multi-Engine TTS**: Support for 5+ engines (LuxTTS, Chatterbox, HumeAI TADA) across 23 languages.  
3. **Audio Workflow**:  
   - **Post-processing**: Apply effects (reverb, pitch shift, compression) via Spotify’s Pedalboard.  
   - **Stories Editor**: DAW-like multi-track timeline for podcasts/audiobooks (arrange clips, mix voices).  
4. **Deployment**:  
   - Desktop: macOS (Apple Silicon/Intel), Windows (MSI/Setup), Linux (coming soon).  
   - Docker: Headless server with web UI.  
   - **Remote Mode**: Run backend on a GPU server (AWS EC2, Vast.ai) while using the desktop client locally.  
5. **OpenAI Compatibility**: Expose OpenAI-compatible endpoints (`/v1/audio/speech`, `/v1/audio/transcriptions`) for drop-in replacement of cloud TTS/ASR.  

### Technical Stack  
- **Frontend**: React, TypeScript, Tauri (desktop shell).  
- **Backend**: FastAPI (Python), SQLAlchemy (SQLite DB).  
- **AI Models**: Qwen3-TTS (cloning), Whisper (ASR), multi-engine TTS.  
- **Tools**: PyInstaller (bundle backend), Docker (deployment), Pedalboard (audio effects).  

### Key Use Cases  
- Podcast/audiobook creation (multi-voice narratives).  
- Game dialogue and video voiceovers.  
- Accessibility (text-to-speech for visually impaired).  
- Local AI voice assistant development.  

Voicebox prioritizes privacy (no cloud data sharing), performance (GPU acceleration), and user control over voice synthesis workflows.
items.summaryZh:
**Voicebox** 是一款开源、优先本地运行的语音克隆与文本转语音（TTS）工具，主打隐私保护，可替代 ElevenLabs 等云端工具。核心功能包括：


### 核心功能  
1. **语音克隆**：通过 Qwen3-TTS 等模型，用 10-30 秒音频片段克隆语音（可捕捉语调与情感）。  
2. **多引擎 TTS**：支持 5 种以上引擎（LuxTTS、Chatterbox、HumeAI TADA），覆盖 23 种语言。  
3. **音频工作流**：  
   - **后期处理**：通过 Spotify 的 Pedalboard 应用效果（混响、音调偏移、压缩）。  
   - **故事编辑器**：类似数字音频工作站（DAW）的多轨时间线，适用于播客/有声书（剪辑排列、语音混音）。  
4. **部署方式**：  
   - 桌面端：macOS（Apple Silicon/Intel）、Windows（MSI 安装包/安装程序）、Linux（即将支持）。  
   - Docker：带网页界面的无头服务器。  
   - **远程模式**：在 GPU 服务器（AWS EC2、Vast.ai）上运行后端，本地使用桌面客户端。  
5. **OpenAI 兼容性**：暴露兼容 OpenAI 的接口（`/v1/audio/speech`、`/v1/audio/transcriptions`），可直接替换云端 TTS/ASR 服务。


### 技术栈  
- **前端**：React、TypeScript、Tauri（桌面端外壳）。  
- **后端**：FastAPI（Python）、SQLAlchemy（SQLite 数据库）。  
- **AI 模型**：Qwen3-TTS（克隆）、Whisper（ASR）、多引擎 TTS。  
- **工具**：PyInstaller（后端打包）、Docker（部署）、Pedalboard（音频效果）。


### 核心应用场景  
- 播客/有声书制作（多语音叙事）。  
- 游戏对话与视频配音。  
- 无障碍服务（视障人士文本转语音）。  
- 本地 AI 语音助手开发。


Voicebox 优先保障隐私（不向云端共享数据）、性能（GPU 加速），并让用户完全掌控语音合成工作流。

items.name: virattt/ai-hedge-fund
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
* **Mission Statement:** An educational proof-of-concept AI hedge fund exploring multi-agent collaboration for AI-driven trading decisions using domain expert strategies.  
* **Target Problem:** Unifies diverse investment approaches (value, growth, macro, risk) into a single system, enabling learning without real-world trading risk.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
* **Core Innovation:** 19+ specialized agents (domain experts like Buffett/Graham + support agents: valuation, risk, portfolio manager) collaborate to generate trading signals.  
* **Comparison:** Unlike single-strategy AI tools, this mimics a human expert team; supports multiple LLMs (OpenAI/Ollama) and financial data APIs.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
* **Key Features:** 1) Multi-agent decision-making; 2) CLI (granular control) + web UI (visual access); 3) Backtesting (historical strategy testing); 4) Multi-LLM/financial API support.
items.summaryZh:
## 1. 项目定位  
* **使命陈述**：本项目为教育类概念验证AI对冲基金，探索多智能体协作在AI驱动交易决策中的应用，依托领域专家策略开展研究。  
* **核心问题**：整合价值投资、成长投资、宏观策略、风险管理等多元投资方法至单一系统，实现无实盘交易风险的学习。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
* **核心创新**：19+个专业智能体（含巴菲特、格雷厄姆等领域专家型智能体，以及估值、风险、投资组合管理等支持型智能体）协作生成交易信号。  
* **对比优势**：区别于单策略AI工具，本项目模拟人类专家团队协作；支持OpenAI、Ollama等多大型语言模型及金融数据API。  

## 3. 实用价值  
* **核心功能**：1) 多智能体决策机制；2) 命令行界面（精细化控制）+ 网页界面（可视化访问）；3) 回测功能（历史策略验证）；4) 多大型语言模型及金融API支持。

items.name: KurtGokhan/tegaki
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
* **Mission Statement:** Tegaki automates converting standard fonts into stroke-by-stroke handwriting animations with natural timing, no manual path authoring or native dependencies.  
* **Target Problem:** Manual handwriting animation path creation is labor-intensive; native dependencies limit cross-framework compatibility.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
* **Core Innovation:** Uses font metadata to generate animated stroke paths automatically (no hand-crafted SVGs).  
* **Comparison:** Unlike manual SVG tools (requires path editing) or framework-specific libraries, Tegaki supports all major frameworks (React, Svelte, Vue, etc.) and works with any font via a web generator.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
* **Key Features:** 1) Framework-agnostic renderer (React, Svelte, Vue, Astro, Web Components); 2) Bundled handwriting fonts + custom font support; 3) No native dependencies; 4) Vanilla JS core with web component wrapper.
items.summaryZh:

* **核心问题：** 手动创建手写动画路径工作量大；原生依赖限制跨框架兼容性。

* **核心创新：** 利用字体元数据自动生成动画笔画路径（无需手动制作SVG）。

## 3. 实用价值

items.name: anthropics/skills
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** Anthropic’s repository of dynamic, self-contained skills to extend Claude’s capabilities for specialized tasks via modular instructions, scripts, and resources.  
**Target Problem:** Claude’s general model lacks task-specific logic (e.g., enterprise workflows, document handling); skills fill this gap without requiring model retraining.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** Modular, on-demand skills (folder + `SKILL.md` file) that extend Claude’s functionality for specific tasks without fine-tuning.  
**Comparison:** Unlike model fine-tuning (static, costly), skills are lightweight, fast to deploy, and accessible via Claude.ai, API, and Claude Code plugins.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. Modular structure (self-contained skill folders with `SKILL.md`).  
2. Multi-access (Claude.ai, API, Claude Code plugins).  
3. Example skills (document handling: PDF/docx; creative/technical tasks).  
4. Custom skill template (simple YAML frontmatter + markdown instructions).
items.summaryZh:
---

## 1. 项目定位  
**使命陈述**：Anthropic的动态独立技能库，借助模块化指令、脚本及资源，扩展Claude在专项任务中的能力。  
**核心问题**：Claude的通用模型缺乏任务特定逻辑（如企业工作流、文档处理）；技能可填补这一空白，无需重新训练模型。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
**核心创新**：模块化按需技能（文件夹+`SKILL.md`文件），无需微调即可扩展Claude在特定任务中的功能。  
**对比**：不同于模型微调（静态、成本高），技能轻量、部署快，可通过Claude.ai、API及Claude Code插件访问。  

## 3. 实用价值  
**核心特性**：  
1. 模块化结构（含`SKILL.md`的独立技能文件夹）  
2. 多渠道访问（Claude.ai、API及Claude Code插件）  
3. 示例技能（文档处理：PDF/docx；创意/技术任务）  
4. 自定义技能模板（简单YAML前置信息+Markdown指令）

items.name: anthropics/claude-code
items.summary:
## 1. Project Identity  
**Mission Statement:** Agentic coding tool that uses natural language to assist with routine tasks, code explanation, and git workflows while understanding the user’s local codebase.  
**Target Problem:** Reduces time on repetitive coding tasks, simplifies complex code comprehension, and streamlines git workflows via integrated, context-aware commands.  

## 2. Innovation & Differentiators  
**Core Innovation:** Agentic task execution (not just suggestions), codebase context awareness, and cross-environment support (terminal, IDE, GitHub @claude tagging).  
**Comparison:** Unlike inline suggestion tools (e.g., Copilot), it offers end-to-end task execution and integrates directly with terminal/IDE workflows and GitHub collaboration.  

## 3. Practical Utility  
**Key Features:**  
1. Natural language command execution for refactoring/debugging.  
2. Context-aware code explanations using local codebase knowledge.  
3. Git workflow assistance (commit messages, branch management).  
4. Plugin extensibility for custom commands/agents.
items.summaryZh:
## 1. 项目定位  
**核心使命**：智能编码工具，依托自然语言交互理解用户本地代码库，辅助完成日常任务、代码解释及Git工作流。  
**核心解决痛点**：通过集成化、上下文感知的命令，减少重复性编码任务耗时，简化复杂代码理解，优化Git工作流。  

## 2. 创新与差异化  
**核心创新**：智能代理式任务执行（而非仅提供建议）、代码库上下文感知、跨环境支持（终端、IDE、GitHub @claude 标签功能）。  
**差异化对比**：不同于Copilot等行内建议工具，本工具支持端到端任务执行，可直接集成终端/IDE工作流及GitHub协作流程。  

## 3. 实用价值  
**核心功能**：  
1. 支持自然语言指令执行，用于代码重构与调试。  
2. 依托本地代码库知识，提供上下文感知的代码解释。  
3. Git工作流辅助（提交信息、分支管理）。  
4. 插件可扩展性，支持自定义指令与代理。
